title: Game Objects — Classes
description: Convert the Player struct to a GameCharacter class with proper encapsulation. Make health and name private members. Add a constructor that takes name and health as parameters. Add public methods: attack() that returns damage dealt, takeDamage(int amount) that reduces health, getHealth() and getName() that return the private values, and isAlive() that returns whether health > 0. Create at least one GameCharacter object for the player and one for an enemy NPC. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
test_code:
assert 'class' in cpp, 'Define a class (e.g., class GameCharacter)'
assert 'private' in cpp, 'Use private access specifier to encapsulate data members'
assert 'public' in cpp, 'Use public access specifier for methods'
import re
constructor = re.search(r'GameCharacter\s*\(', cpp)
assert constructor, 'Define a constructor for GameCharacter (e.g., GameCharacter(string n, int h, int ap))'
assert 'getHealth' in cpp or 'get_health' in cpp, 'Add a getter method for health'
assert 'getName' in cpp or 'get_name' in cpp, 'Add a getter method for name'
assert 'takeDamage' in cpp or 'take_damage' in cpp, 'Add a takeDamage method'
assert 'attack' in cpp, 'Add an attack method'
assert 'isAlive' in cpp or 'is_alive' in cpp, 'Add an isAlive method that checks if health > 0'
acceptance_criteria: GameCharacter class defined with private name, health, and attackPower
Constructor takes parameters to initialize the character
Public methods: attack(), takeDamage(), getHealth(), getName(), isAlive()
Getter methods provide access to private members
At least one player and one enemy GameCharacter created
Combat uses attack() and takeDamage() methods
hints: GameCharacter::GameCharacter(string n, int h, int ap) : name(n), health(h), attackPower(ap) {}
void GameCharacter::takeDamage(int amount) { health -= amount; if (health < 0) health = 0; }
Use const after method declarations that don't modify the object: int getHealth() const;
